generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance AirQualitySensorNetwork : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi-based sensor network deployed for real-time environmental monitoring in Air Quality Management exemplar. Collects multiple air quality metrics including CO2, particulate matter, and humidity."
    DTDFVocab:producedData AirQualityDataTransmitted
]

instance GreenhouseEnvironmentalSensors : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor network integrated into GreenhouseDT system for environmental monitoring, though specific sensor types are not detailed. Enables data-driven behavioral adaptation of the digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GreenhouseEnvironmentalDataTransmitted
]

instance CranePositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position tracking sensor for the lab-scale gantry crane, capturing joint angles, payload location, velocity, and acceleration with millisecond-level resolution."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneStateDataTransmitted
]

instance CraneLoadCell : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Force/torque measurement sensor for the gantry crane payload, providing real-time load monitoring for safety and control validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneLoadDataTransmitted
]

instance CraneControllerSignals : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Controller interface capturing actuator signals (e.g., motor commands, encoder feedback) for trajectory execution monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ControllerSignalDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance AirQualityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AirQualitySensorNetwork
    base:desc "Real-time air quality metrics transmitted from Raspberry Pi sensor network to digital twin pipeline via direct Python-based connection."
]

instance GreenhouseEnvironmentalDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GreenhouseEnvironmentalSensors
    base:desc "Environmental sensor data from greenhouse system transmitted to digital twin behavioral models for self-adaptation."
]

instance CraneStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CranePositionSensor
    base:desc "High-frequency state variables (position, velocity, acceleration) transmitted from physical crane to logging application via TCP/IP or serial communication."
]

instance CraneLoadDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneLoadCell
    base:desc "Force/torque measurements from payload transmitted in real-time for load monitoring and safety validation."
]

instance ControllerSignalDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneControllerSignals
    base:desc "Controller output signals transmitted for trajectory execution validation and closed-loop control synchronization."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance AutomationMLAirQualityModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AutomationML-based structured representation of physical and logical components for Air Quality Management exemplar, enabling standardized digital twin implementation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AzureDigitalTwinPipeline, AutomationMLSimulationEngine
]

instance GreenhouseBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model for both physical greenhouse and its digital twin, facilitating self-adaptive responses to environmental changes."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActiveMQMessageBroker
]

instance CraneKinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Kinematic model of gantry crane defined using Denavit-Hartenberg parameters, implemented in MATLAB/Simulink or Python (PyBullet/PyRos) for forward/inverse kinematics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROSPhysicsSimulation, TrajectoryGeneratorService
]

instance CraneDynamicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic model capturing payload effects, actuator constraints, and environmental interactions for accurate simulation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROSPhysicsSimulation
]

instance AirQualityTimeSeriesData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical and real-time air quality measurements stored in structured format (CSV/SQL) with millisecond precision for post-hoc analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AirQualityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo InfluxDBTimeSeriesDatabase
]

instance GreenhouseEnvironmentalTimeSeriesData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series environmental data from greenhouse sensors stored in InfluxDB for behavioral adaptation analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GreenhouseEnvironmentalDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo InfluxDBTimeSeriesDatabase
]

instance CraneStateTimeSeriesData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Millisecond-resolution state data (position, velocity, acceleration) stored in TimescaleDB for trajectory analysis and validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneStateDataTransmitted, ControllerSignalDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBDatabase
]

instance CraneLoadTimeSeriesData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Force/torque measurements stored for load monitoring and fault detection analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneLoadDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBDatabase
]

// C11: Enablers
instance AutomationMLSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation engine processing AutomationML models for air quality scenario testing and prediction."
    DTDFVocab:enables AirQualityPredictionService
]

instance ActiveMQMessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time message broker facilitating data exchange between physical greenhouse and digital twin components."
    DTDFVocab:enables GreenhouseAdaptationService, GreenhouseVisualizationService
]

instance AzureDigitalTwinPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Microsoft Azure-based pipeline for air quality digital twin implementation, including data processing and cloud services."

]

instance ROSPhysicsSimulation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS-based physics simulation engine (Gazebo/CoppeliaSim) for real-time crane behavior simulation and validation."

]

    base:desc "ROS Control framework implementing closed-loop control algorithms (PID, MPC) for crane actuation."
]

instance TimescaleDBDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database storing high-resolution crane state and load data for historical analysis."
]

instance InfluxDBTimeSeriesDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database for environmental and air quality data storage with timestamp precision."
]

    base:desc "3D visualization tool (ROS RViz) for real-time crane state overlay and trajectory visualization."
]

    base:desc "Customizable dashboard for real-time monitoring of air quality and greenhouse environmental metrics."
]

instance MATLABSystemIdentification : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "System identification toolbox for model validation and parameter tuning between physical and digital systems."
]

instance ScikitLearnFaultDetection : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Machine learning library for anomaly detection and fault classification in crane operation."
]

    base:desc "Graph-based notation for documenting system evolution (hardware upgrades, control retuning) in digital twin lifecycle."
]

// C6: Services
instance OptimalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time trajectory optimization service generating energy-efficient crane maneuvers using kinematic models and model-predictive control."

]

instance FaultTolerantControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [

]

]

]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in this Digital Twin system is mediated by the **Control Application**, which generates and transmits control commands to the **Physical Crane** via the **Physical Crane Interface**. Key aspects include:
  - **Command types**: Movement trajectories (e.g., optimal paths generated by the **Trajectory Generator Service**) and operational directives (e.g., start/stop signals).
  - **Control message formats**: Likely structured as **Python objects or serialized data** (e.g., JSON or binary payloads) containing waypoints, velocity profiles, and timing constraints.
  - **Validation and authentication**: Not explicitly detailed, but the **Continuous Validation Service** ensures that executed trajectories match simulations, implying a **closed-loop validation** mechanism. The **Control Application** likely enforces basic sanity checks (e.g., crane limits) before issuing commands.
  - **Feedback systems**: The **Physical Crane Interface** provides real-time feedback (e.g., sensor data, error codes) to the **Control Application**, enabling adaptive control. The **Logging Application** further logs these interactions for post-mortem analysis.
  - **Conflict resolution**: Not explicitly described, but the system’s modular architecture (e.g., **MQTT Broker** for service communication) suggests **prioritization of commands** based on service origin (e.g., operator overrides via **Human Machine Interface** take precedence over automated trajectories).
  - **Command execution confirmation**: Implied via the **Continuous Validation Service**, which compares simulated and executed trajectories. If discrepancies exceed thresholds, operators are **notified via the Grafana dashboard** for manual intervention."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin systems in the documents follow an **ad-hoc, component-based orchestration** rather than a standardized framework. Key characteristics include:
  - **GreenhouseDT**: Uses **InfluxDB** (time-series database) and **ActiveMQ** (message broker) for **event-driven synchronization** between physical (greenhouse) and digital components. The system relies on **virtual machines** with **prerecorded data**, suggesting **offline-capable or batch-processed adaptation** rather than real-time orchestration.
  - **Air Quality Management**: Implements a **pipeline on Microsoft Azure** with **AutomationML-based digital twin modeling**, integrating **Raspberry Pi sensor data** via an unspecified protocol. The **three applications (runtime visualization, simulation, prediction)** imply a **modular service architecture** but lack explicit details on **dynamic resource allocation** or **fault tolerance**.
  - **Gantry Crane**: Described using **C4 Model** (context/container diagrams), indicating a **software-centric architecture** with potential for **microservices or containerized components** (e.g., via **containerization/virtualization**), but no specifics on **orchestration frameworks** (e.g., Kubernetes, Docker Swarm) or **inter-service communication protocols** are provided. The **ad-hoc approach** avoids commercial frameworks, favoring **custom integration** of physical (crane) and digital layers."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **Air Quality Management** exemplar emphasizes **runtime validation** through visualization, simulation, and prediction applications, though no explicit fidelity metrics (e.g., error thresholds, model accuracy) are quantified. The **GreenhouseDT** focuses on **self-adaptive behavioral fidelity**, with prerecorded data in virtual machines suggesting pre-validation of baseline models. Uncertainty sources (e.g., sensor noise, model drift) are not addressed, nor are error correction mechanisms. **Quality assurance** appears ad-hoc, relying on domain-specific adaptations rather than standardized validation protocols. The **AutomationML-based digital twin** implies structured data integrity but lacks formal verification methods."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin’s technical network infrastructure** relies on a **service-oriented architecture** with the following key elements:
  - **Communication protocols**:
    - **MQTT Broker**: Used for lightweight, asynchronous messaging between services (e.g., **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**, **Continuous Validation Service**). MQTT’s publish-subscribe model ensures **low-overhead, event-driven communication**.
    - **Database connectivity**: **TimescaleDB** (a time-series database extension of PostgreSQL) is accessed via standard **SQL/PostgreSQL protocols** for structured data storage and retrieval.
    - **Service-to-service**: Likely **HTTP/REST or gRPC** for synchronous interactions (e.g., **Control Application** querying the **Trajectory Generator Service**).
  - **Network topology and architecture**: A **star topology** centered around the **MQTT Broker**, with services (e.g., **Dashboard**, **Simulation Service**) subscribing to relevant topics. The **Physical Crane Interface** acts as a bridge between the physical and digital layers, likely using **dedicated serial/TCP links** for real-time control.
  - **Bandwidth and latency requirements**:
    - **Control loop**: Low-latency (<100ms) for **Physical Crane Interface** ↔ **Control Application** communication to ensure responsive operation.
    - **Data logging**: Higher throughput for **Logging Application** ↔ **TimescaleDB** (optimized for time-series data ingestion).
    - **Simulation/validation**: Moderate latency for **Simulation Service** ↔ **Continuous Validation Service** comparisons.
  - **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms**:
    - **MQTT QoS levels** (e.g., QoS 1 or 2) to ensure message delivery for critical commands.
    - **TimescaleDB replication** (not explicitly stated but implied for high availability).
    - **Fallback modes**: The **Physical Crane** can operate independently of the Digital Twin if needed.
  - **Security protocols**: Not explicitly detailed, but best practices would include:
    - **MQTT over TLS** for encrypted service communication.
    - **Database authentication** (e.g., PostgreSQL roles) for secure access to **TimescaleDB**.
    - **API key or JWT-based authentication** for the **Human Machine Interface** and **Dashboard**."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin’s hosting and deployment infrastructure** is designed as a **hybrid on-premise/edge system** with the following specifications:
  - **Hosting platforms**:
    - **On-premise**: The **Physical Crane**, **Physical Crane Interface**, **Control Application**, **Logging Application**, and **Human Machine Interface** are deployed locally to minimize latency for real-time control.
    - **Edge computing**: Likely used for **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**, and **Continuous Validation Service** to reduce dependency on cloud resources for time-sensitive operations.
    - **Cloud (implied)**: The **TimescaleDB database** and **Grafana Dashboard** may leverage cloud or on-premise servers for scalability, though the document does not specify.
  - **Computational and storage requirements**:
    - **CPU/GPU**: Moderate requirements for **Trajectory Generation** (kinematics modeling) and **Simulation** (physics-based modeling of crane dynamics).
    - **Memory**: Optimized for real-time data streams (e.g., **Logging Application** buffering sensor data).
    - **Storage**: **TimescaleDB** handles time-series data efficiently, with compression for long-term storage.
  - **Scalability mechanisms**:
    - **Modular services**: Each container (e.g., **Trajectory Generator Service**) can be scaled independently based on demand.
    - **Database partitioning**: **TimescaleDB** supports horizontal scaling for large datasets.
  - **Deployment models supported**:
    - **Containerized microservices**: All Digital Twin components (except the **Physical Crane**) are deployed as **Python-based services**, likely using lightweight containers (e.g., Docker, though not explicitly stated).
    - **Hybrid deployment**: Critical services (e.g., **Control Application**) run on-premise, while analytics (e.g., **Dashboard**) may be cloud-hosted.
  - **Availability/reliability assurance**:
    - **Redundant logging**: The **Logging Application** ensures no data loss during crane operation.
    - **Fallback to physical-only mode**: The **Physical Crane** can operate autonomously if the Digital Twin fails.
    - **Monitoring**: The **Grafana Dashboard** provides real-time visibility into system health and alerts for anomalies (e.g., trajectory validation failures)."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin systems exhibit **limited horizontal integration** with external systems, relying on **domain-specific or proprietary solutions**:
  - **GreenhouseDT**: Integrates with **InfluxDB** (time-series storage) and **ActiveMQ** (message broker) but lacks explicit **interoperability standards** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) for broader system integration. The **virtual machine-based deployment** suggests **self-contained operation** with no mention of **enterprise system dependencies**.
  - **Air Quality Management**: Uses **AutomationML** for digital twin modeling and **Microsoft Azure tools** for pipeline implementation, implying **cloud-native integration** but no details on **standardized data exchange protocols** (e.g., OData, REST APIs). The **Raspberry Pi sensor layer** may interface with **local IoT platforms**, but no external system dependencies are specified.
  - **Gantry Crane**: The **C4 Model** suggests a **software-focused architecture**, but no **external system integrations** (e.g., ERP, SCADA, or other Digital Twins) are described. The **cyber-physical system (CPS) context** hints at potential for **industrial protocols** (e.g., PROFINET, EtherCAT), though these are not mentioned. The **ad-hoc approach** prioritizes **internal cohesion** over **horizontal scalability** or **cross-system interoperability**."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The **Air Quality Management** exemplar adheres to **AutomationML** for digital twin modeling, a standardized format for **engineering data exchange** in automation and cyber-physical systems. The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar does not explicitly mention standardization but relies on **commonly used technologies** (InfluxDB, ActiveMQ) with no industry-specific certifications or compliance frameworks cited. Neither exemplar references **interoperability standards** (e.g., OPC UA, FIWARE) or **domain-specific certifications** (e.g., ISO 27001 for cybersecurity, IEC 62443 for industrial systems). The **ad-hoc approach** of both systems implies limited adherence to **formal compliance frameworks**, though **Microsoft Azure tools** (used in Air Quality Management) may inherently support cloud-based standardization practices (e.g., Azure IoT Hub protocols). No evidence of **certification requirements** or **validation against industry benchmarks** is provided."
]
